steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: “the Olympic committee once decided ______ tug-of-war as an official Olympic event.” The blank must be filled with a verb form that completes the thought after the past-tense verb “decided.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for verb 'decide'
steps.content: The verb “decide” requires an infinitive (to + base verb). The correct answer is “to include,” producing “decided to include tug-of-war.” The other three choices fail because they're the wrong verb form: “include” lacks the “to,” “including” has -ing without “to,” and “included” is past tense. This matches choice D — D is correct.
